header.title: Listen - Hear - Act
header.description: These are some references relating to listening, hearing, action and practice.

blocks.title: Parable of the Sower
blocks.description: This mentions those who hear and don't listen

texts.NIV84: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the lake.
texts.KJV: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.
texts.NASB95: That day Jesus went out of the house and was sitting by the sea.
texts.NKJV: On the same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.
texts.DRB: THE same day Jesus going out of the house, sat by the sea side.

texts.NIV84: Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat in it, while all the people stood on the shore.
texts.KJV: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
texts.NASB95: And large crowds gathered to Him, so He got into a boat and sat down, and the whole crowd was standing on the beach.
texts.NKJV: And great multitudes were gathered together to Him, so that He got into a boat and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
texts.DRB: And great multitudes were gathered unto him, so that he went up into a boat and sat: and all the multitude stood on the shore.

texts.NIV84: Then he told them many things in parables, saying: "A farmer went out to sow his seed.
texts.KJV: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
texts.NASB95: And He spoke many things to them in parables, saying, “Behold, the sower went out to sow;
texts.NKJV: Then He spoke many things to them in parables, saying: “Behold, a sower went out to sow.
texts.DRB: And he spoke to them many things in parables, saying: Behold the sower went forth to sow.

texts.NIV84: As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
texts.KJV: And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
texts.NASB95: and as he sowed, some seeds fell beside the road, and the birds came and ate them up.
texts.NKJV: And as he sowed, some seed fell by the wayside; and the birds came and devoured them.
texts.DRB: And whilst he soweth some fell by the way side, and the birds of the air came and ate them up.

texts.NIV84: Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.
texts.KJV: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
texts.NASB95: Others fell on the rocky places, where they did not have much soil; and immediately they sprang up, because they had no depth of soil.
texts.NKJV: Some fell on stony places, where they did not have much earth; and they immediately sprang up because they had no depth of earth.
texts.DRB: And other some fell upon stony ground, where they had not much earth: and they sprung up immediately, because they had no deepness of earth.

texts.NIV84: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
texts.KJV: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
texts.NASB95: But when the sun had risen, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
texts.NKJV: But when the sun was up they were scorched, and because they had no root they withered away.
texts.DRB: And when the sun was up they were scorched: and because they had not root, they withered away.

texts.NIV84: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants.
texts.KJV: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
texts.NASB95: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns came up and choked them out.
texts.NKJV: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns sprang up and choked them.
texts.DRB: And others fell among thorns: and the thorns grew up and choked them.

texts.NIV84: Still other seed fell on good soil, where it produced a crop--a hundred, sixty or thirty times what was sown.
texts.KJV: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
texts.NASB95: And others fell on the good soil and yielded a crop, some a hundredfold, some sixty, and some thirty.
texts.NKJV: But others fell on good ground and yielded a crop: some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
texts.DRB: And others fell upon good ground: and they brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, and some thirtyfold.

texts.NIV84: He who has ears, let him hear."
texts.KJV: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
texts.NASB95: He who has ears, let him hear.”
texts.NKJV: He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”
texts.DRB: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

blocks.title: Parable of the Sower
blocks.description: This mentions those who hear and don't listen

texts.NIV84: Again Jesus began to teach by the lake. The crowd that gathered around him was so large that he got into a boat and sat in it out on the lake, while all the people were along the shore at the water's edge.
texts.KJV: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
texts.NASB95: He began to teach again by the sea. And such a very large crowd gathered to Him that He got into a boat in the sea and sat down; and the whole crowd was by the sea on the land.
texts.NKJV: And again He began to teach by the sea. And a great multitude was gathered to Him, so that He got into a boat and sat in it on the sea; and the whole multitude was on the land facing the sea.
texts.DRB: AND again he began to teach by the sea side; and a great multitude was gathered together unto him, so that he went up into a ship, and sat in the sea; and all the multitude was upon the land by the sea side.

texts.NIV84: He taught them many things by parables, and in his teaching said:
texts.KJV: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
texts.NASB95: And He was teaching them many things in parables, and was saying to them in His teaching,
texts.NKJV: Then He taught them many things by parables, and said to them in His teaching:
texts.DRB: And he taught them many things in parables, and said unto them in his doctrine:

texts.NIV84: "Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.
texts.KJV: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
texts.NASB95: “Listen to this! Behold, the sower went out to sow;
texts.NKJV: “Listen! Behold, a sower went out to sow.
texts.DRB: Hear ye: Behold, the sower went out to sow.

texts.NIV84: As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
texts.KJV: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
texts.NASB95: as he was sowing, some seed fell beside the road, and the birds came and ate it up.
texts.NKJV: And it happened, as he sowed, that some seed fell by the wayside; and the birds of the air came and devoured it.
texts.DRB: And whilst he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the birds of the air came and ate it up.

texts.NIV84: Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.
texts.KJV: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
texts.NASB95: Other seed fell on the rocky ground where it did not have much soil; and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of soil.
texts.NKJV: Some fell on stony ground, where it did not have much earth; and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of earth.
texts.DRB: And other some fell upon stony ground, where it had not much earth; and it shot up immediately, because it had no depth of earth.

texts.NIV84: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
texts.KJV: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
texts.NASB95: And after the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
texts.NKJV: But when the sun was up it was scorched, and because it had no root it withered away.
texts.DRB: And when the sun was risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

texts.NIV84: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants, so that they did not bear grain.
texts.KJV: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
texts.NASB95: Other seed fell among the thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and it yielded no crop.
texts.NKJV: And some seed fell among thorns; and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no crop.
texts.DRB: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

texts.NIV84: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up, grew and produced a crop, multiplying thirty, sixty, or even a hundred times."
texts.KJV: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
texts.NASB95: Other seeds fell into the good soil, and as they grew up and increased, they yielded a crop and produced thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.”
texts.NKJV: But other seed fell on good ground and yielded a crop that sprang up, increased and produced: some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some a hundred.”
texts.DRB: And some fell upon good ground; and brought forth fruit that grew up, and increased and yielded, one thirty, another sixty, and another a hundred.

texts.NIV84: Then Jesus said, "He who has ears to hear, let him hear."
texts.KJV: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
texts.NASB95: And He was saying, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
texts.NKJV: And He said to them, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”
texts.DRB: And he said: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

blocks.title: Parable of the Sower
blocks.description: This mentions those who hear and don't listen

texts.NIV84: While a large crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from town after town, he told this parable:
texts.KJV: And when much people were gathered together, and were come to him out of every city, he spake by a parable:
texts.NASB95: When a large crowd was coming together, and those from the various cities were journeying to Him, He spoke by way of a parable:
texts.NKJV: And when a great multitude had gathered, and they had come to Him from every city, He spoke by a parable:
texts.DRB: And when a very great multitude was gathered together, and hastened out of the cities unto him, he spoke by a similitude.

texts.NIV84: "A farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path; it was trampled on, and the birds of the air ate it up.
texts.KJV: A sower went out to sow his seed: and as he sowed, some fell by the way side; and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.
texts.NASB95: “The sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell beside the road, and it was trampled under foot and the birds of the air ate it up.
texts.NKJV: “A sower went out to sow his seed. And as he sowed, some fell by the wayside; and it was trampled down, and the birds of the air devoured it.
texts.DRB: The sower went out to sow his seed. And as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the air devoured it.

texts.NIV84: Some fell on rock, and when it came up, the plants withered because they had no moisture.
texts.KJV: And some fell upon a rock; and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it lacked moisture.
texts.NASB95: Other seed fell on rocky soil, and as soon as it grew up, it withered away, because it had no moisture.
texts.NKJV: Some fell on rock; and as soon as it sprang up, it withered away because it lacked moisture.
texts.DRB: And other some fell upon a rock: and as soon as it was sprung up, it withered away, because it had no moisture.

texts.NIV84: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up with it and choked the plants.
texts.KJV: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprang up with it, and choked it.
texts.NASB95: Other seed fell among the thorns; and the thorns grew up with it and choked it out.
texts.NKJV: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns sprang up with it and choked it.
texts.DRB: And other some fell among thorns, and the thorns growing up with it, choked it.

texts.NIV84: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up and yielded a crop, a hundred times more than was sown." When he said this, he called out, "He who has ears to hear, let him hear."
texts.KJV: And other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold. And when he had said these things, he cried, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
texts.NASB95: Other seed fell into the good soil, and grew up, and produced a crop a hundred times as great.” As He said these things, He would call out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
texts.NKJV: But others fell on good ground, sprang up, and yielded a crop a hundredfold.” When He had said these things He cried, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”
texts.DRB: And other some fell upon good ground; and being sprung up, yielded fruit a hundredfold. Saying these things, he cried out: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

texts.NIV84: The disciples came to him and asked, "Why do you speak to the people in parables?"
texts.KJV: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?
texts.NASB95: And the disciples came and said to Him, “Why do You speak to them in parables?”
texts.NKJV: And the disciples came and said to Him, “Why do You speak to them in parables?”
texts.DRB: And his disciples came and said to him: Why speakest thou to them in parables?

texts.NIV84: He replied, "The knowledge of the secrets of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them.
texts.KJV: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
texts.NASB95: Jesus answered them, “To you it has been granted to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been granted.
texts.NKJV: He answered and said to them, “Because it has been given to you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given.
texts.DRB: Who answered and said to them: Because to you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven: but to them it is not given.

texts.NIV84: Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.
texts.KJV: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
texts.NASB95: For whoever has, to him more shall be given, and he will have an abundance; but whoever does not have, even what he has shall be taken away from him.
texts.NKJV: For whoever has, to him more will be given, and he will have abundance; but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
texts.DRB: For he that hath, to him shall be given, and he shall abound: but he that hath not, from him shall be taken away that also which he hath.

texts.NIV84: This is why I speak to them in parables: "Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.
texts.KJV: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
texts.NASB95: Therefore I speak to them in parables; because while seeing they do not see, and while hearing they do not hear, nor do they understand.
texts.NKJV: Therefore I speak to them in parables, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor do they understand.
texts.DRB: Therefore do I speak to them in parables: because seeing they see not, and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.

texts.NIV84: In them is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah: "'You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.
texts.KJV: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:
texts.NASB95: In their case the prophecy of Isaiah is being fulfilled, which says, ‘You will keep on hearing, but will not understand; You will keep on seeing, but will not perceive;
texts.NKJV: And in them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled, which says: ‘Hearing you will hear and shall not understand, And seeing you will see and not perceive;
texts.DRB: And the prophecy of Isaias is fulfilled in them, who saith: By hearing you shall hear, and shall not understand: and seeing you shall see, and shall not perceive.

texts.NIV84: For this people's heart has become calloused; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts and turn, and I would heal them.'
texts.KJV: For this people's heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
texts.NASB95: For the heart of this people has become dull, With their ears they scarcely hear, And they have closed their eyes, Otherwise they would see with their eyes, Hear with their ears, And understand with their heart and return, And I would heal them.’
texts.NKJV: For the hearts of this people have grown dull. Their ears are hard of hearing, And their eyes they have closed, Lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears, Lest they should understand with their hearts and turn, So that I should heal them.’
texts.DRB: For the heart of this people is grown gross, and with their ears they have been dull of hearing, and their eyes they have shut: lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.

texts.NIV84: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.
texts.KJV: But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.
texts.NASB95: But blessed are your eyes, because they see; and your ears, because they hear.
texts.NKJV: But blessed are your eyes for they see, and your ears for they hear;
texts.DRB: But blessed are your eyes, because they see, and your ears, because they hear.

texts.NIV84: For I tell you the truth, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.
texts.KJV: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
texts.NASB95: For truly I say to you that many prophets and righteous men desired to see what you see, and did not see it, and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.
texts.NKJV: for assuredly, I say to you that many prophets and righteous men desired to see what you see, and did not see it, and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.
texts.DRB: For, amen, I say to you, many prophets and just men have desired to see the things that you see, and have not seen them, and to hear the things that you hear and have not heard them.

texts.NIV84: When he was alone, the Twelve and the others around him asked him about the parables.
texts.KJV: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
texts.NASB95: As soon as He was alone, His followers, along with the twelve, began asking Him about the parables.
texts.NKJV: But when He was alone, those around Him with the twelve asked Him about the parable.
texts.DRB: And when he was alone, the twelve that were with him asked him the parable.

texts.NIV84: He told them, "The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those on the outside everything is said in parables
texts.KJV: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:
texts.NASB95: And He was saying to them, <span style='color:#952004; font-weight:600'>“To you has been given the mystery of the kingdom of God, but those who are outside get everything in parables,>/span>
texts.NKJV: And He said to them, “To you it has been given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God; but to those who are outside, all things come in parables,
texts.DRB: And he said to them: To you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but to them that are without, all things are done in parables:

texts.NIV84: so that, "'they may be ever seeing but never perceiving, and ever hearing but never understanding; otherwise they might turn and be forgiven!'"
texts.KJV: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.
texts.NASB95: so that while seeing, they may see and not perceive, and while hearing, they may hear and not understand, otherwise they might return and be forgiven.”
texts.NKJV: so that ‘Seeing they may see and not perceive, And hearing they may hear and not understand; Lest they should turn, And their sins be forgiven them.’ ”
texts.DRB: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand: lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.

texts.NIV84: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
texts.KJV: And his disciples asked him, saying, What might this parable be?
texts.NASB95: His disciples began questioning Him as to what this parable meant.
texts.NKJV: Then His disciples asked Him, saying, “What does this parable mean?”
texts.DRB: And his disciples asked him what this parable might be.

texts.NIV84: He said, "The knowledge of the secrets of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to others I speak in parables, so that, "'though seeing, they may not see; though hearing, they may not understand.'
texts.KJV: And he said, Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.
texts.NASB95: And He said, “To you it has been granted to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God, but to the rest it is in parables, so that seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.
texts.NKJV: And He said, “To you it has been given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God, but to the rest it is given in parables, that ‘Seeing they may not see, And hearing they may not understand.’
texts.DRB: To whom he said: To you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God; but to the rest in parables, that seeing they may not see, and hearing may not understand.

texts.NIV84: He said, "Go and tell this people: "'Be ever hearing, but never understanding; be ever seeing, but never perceiving.'
texts.KJV: And he said, Go, and tell this people, Hear ye indeed, but understand not; and see ye indeed, but perceive not.
texts.DRB: And he said: Go, and thou shalt say to this people: Hearing, hear, and understand not: and see the vision, and know it not.

texts.NIV84: Make the heart of this people calloused; make their ears dull and close their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn and be healed."
texts.KJV: Make the heart of this people fat, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes; lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and convert, and be healed.
texts.NASB95:
“Render the hearts of this people insensitive,
Their ears dull,
And their eyes dim,
Otherwise they might see with their eyes,
Hear with their ears,
Understand with their hearts,
And return and be healed.”
texts.NKJV:
“Make the heart of this people dull,
And their ears heavy,
And shut their eyes;
Lest they see with their eyes,
And hear with their ears,
And understand with their heart,
And return and be healed.”
texts.DRB: Blind the heart of this people, and make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes: lest they see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and be converted and I heal them.

texts.NIV84: For this people's heart has become calloused; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts and turn, and I would heal them.'
texts.KJV: For the heart of this people is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes have they closed; lest they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
texts.NASB95:
For the heart of this people has become dull,
And with their ears they scarcely hear,
And they have closed their eyes;
Otherwise they might see with their eyes,
And hear with their ears,
And understand with their heart and return,
And I would heal them.”’
texts.NKJV:
For the hearts of this people have grown dull.
Their ears are hard of hearing,
And their eyes they have closed,
Lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears,
Lest they should understand with their hearts and turn,
So that I should heal them.” ’
texts.DRB: For the heart of this people is grown gross, and with their ears have they heard heavily, and their eyes they have shut; lest perhaps they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

texts.NIV84: "Listen then to what the parable of the sower means:
texts.KJV: Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.
texts.NASB95: “Hear then the parable of the sower.
texts.NKJV: “Therefore hear the parable of the sower:
texts.DRB: Hear you therefore the parable of the sower.

texts.NIV84: When anyone hears the message about the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown along the path.
texts.KJV: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
texts.NASB95: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what has been sown in his heart. This is the one on whom seed was sown beside the road.
texts.NKJV: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom, and does not understand it, then the wicked one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is he who received seed by the wayside.
texts.DRB: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth it not, there cometh the wicked one, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart: this is he that received the seed by the way side.

texts.NIV84: The one who received the seed that fell on rocky places is the man who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.
texts.KJV: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;
texts.NASB95: The one on whom seed was sown on the rocky places, this is the man who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy;
texts.NKJV: But he who received the seed on stony places, this is he who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy;
texts.DRB: And he that received the seed upon stony ground, is he that heareth the word, and immediately receiveth it with joy.

texts.NIV84: But since he has no root, he lasts only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.
texts.KJV: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
texts.NASB95: yet he has no firm root in himself, but is only temporary, and when affliction or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he falls away.
texts.NKJV: yet he has no root in himself, but endures only for a while. For when tribulation or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he stumbles.
texts.DRB: Yet hath he not root in himself, but is only for a time: and when there ariseth tribulation and persecution because of the word, he is presently scandalized.

texts.NASB95: And the one on whom seed was sown among the thorns, this is the man who hears the word, and the worry of the world and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
texts.NKJV: Now he who received seed among the thorns is he who hears the word, and the cares of this world and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and he becomes unfruitful.

texts.NIV84: But the one who received the seed that fell on good soil is the man who hears the word and understands it. He produces a crop, yielding a hundred, sixty or thirty times what was sown."

texts.NKJV: But he who received seed on the good ground is he who hears the word and understands it, who indeed bears fruit and produces: some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.”
texts.DRB: But he that received the seed upon good ground, is he that heareth the word, and understandeth, and beareth fruit, and yieldeth the one an hundredfold, and another sixty, and another thirty.

texts.NIV84: Then Jesus said to them, "Don't you understand this parable? How then will you understand any parable?
texts.KJV: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
texts.NASB95: And He said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? How will you understand all the parables?
texts.NKJV: And He said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? How then will you understand all the parables?
texts.DRB: And he saith to them: Are you ignorant of this parable? and how shall you know all parables?

texts.NIV84: The farmer sows the word.
texts.KJV: The sower soweth the word.
texts.NASB95: The sower sows the word.
texts.NKJV: The sower sows the word.
texts.DRB: He that soweth, soweth the word.

texts.NIV84: Some people are like seed along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
texts.KJV: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
texts.NASB95: These are the ones who are beside the road where the word is sown; and when they hear, immediately Satan comes and takes away the word which has been sown in them.
texts.NKJV: And these are the ones by the wayside where the word is sown. When they hear, Satan comes immediately and takes away the word that was sown in their hearts.
texts.DRB: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown, and as soon as they have heard, immediately Satan cometh and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.

texts.NIV84: Others, like seed sown on rocky places, hear the word and at once receive it with joy.
texts.KJV: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
texts.NASB95: In a similar way these are the ones on whom seed was sown on the rocky places, who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy;
texts.NKJV: These likewise are the ones sown on stony ground who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
texts.DRB: And these likewise are they that are sown on the stony ground: who when they have heard the word, immediately recieve it with joy.

texts.NIV84: But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
texts.KJV: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word's sake, immediately they are offended.
texts.NASB95: and they have no firm root in themselves, but are only temporary; then, when affliction or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they fall away.
texts.NKJV: and they have no root in themselves, and so endure only for a time. Afterward, when tribulation or persecution arises for the word’s sake, immediately they stumble.
texts.DRB: And they have no root in themselves, but are only for a time: and then when tribulation and persecution ariseth for the word they are presently scandalized.

texts.NIV84: Still others, like seed sown among thorns, hear the word;
texts.KJV: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
texts.NASB95: And others are the ones on whom seed was sown among the thorns; these are the ones who have heard the word,
texts.NKJV: Now these are the ones sown among thorns; they are the ones who hear the word,
texts.DRB: And others there are who are sown among thorns: these are they that hear the word,

texts.NIV84: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.

texts.NASB95: but the worries of the world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

texts.DRB: And the cares of the world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts after other things entering in choke the word, and it is made fruitless.

texts.NIV84: Others, like seed sown on good soil, hear the word, accept it, and produce a crop--thirty, sixty or even a hundred times what was sown."
texts.KJV: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.

texts.NKJV: But these are the ones sown on good ground, those who hear the word, accept it, and bear fruit: some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some a hundred.”
texts.DRB: And these are they who are sown upon the good ground, who hear the word, and receive it, and yield fruit, the one thirty, another sixty, and another a hundred.

texts.NIV84: "This is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God.
texts.KJV: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
texts.NASB95: “Now the parable is this: the seed is the word of God.
texts.NKJV: “Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
texts.DRB: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.

texts.NIV84: Those along the path are the ones who hear, and then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
texts.KJV: Those by the way side are they that hear; then cometh the devil, and taketh away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.
texts.NASB95: Those beside the road are those who have heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their heart, so that they will not believe and be saved.
texts.NKJV: Those by the wayside are the ones who hear; then the devil comes and takes away the word out of their hearts, lest they should believe and be saved.
texts.DRB: And they by the way side are they that hear; then the devil cometh, and taketh the word out of their heart, lest believing they should be saved.

texts.NIV84: Those on the rock are the ones who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while, but in the time of testing they fall away.
texts.KJV: They on the rock are they, which, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, which for a while believe, and in time of temptation fall away.
texts.NASB95: Those on the rocky soil are those who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no firm root; they believe for a while, and in time of temptation fall away.
texts.NKJV: But the ones on the rock are those who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, who believe for a while and in time of temptation fall away.
texts.DRB: Now they upon the rock, are they who when they hear, receive the word with joy: and these have no roots; for they believe for a while, and in time of temptation, they fall away.

texts.NIV84: The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by life's worries, riches and pleasures, and they do not mature.
texts.KJV: And that which fell among thorns are they, which, when they have heard, go forth, and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and bring no fruit to perfection.

texts.DRB: And that which fell among thorns, are they who have heard, and going their way, are choked with the cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and yield no fruit.

texts.NIV84: But the seed on good soil stands for those with a noble and good heart, who hear the word, retain it, and by persevering produce a crop.
texts.KJV: But that on the good ground are they, which in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.
texts.NASB95: But the seed in the good soil, these are the ones who have heard the word in an honest and good heart, and hold it fast, and bear fruit with perseverance.
texts.NKJV: But the ones that fell on the good ground are those who, having heard the word with a noble and good heart, keep it and bear fruit with patience.
texts.DRB: But that on the good ground, are they who in a good and perfect heart, hearing the word, keep it, and bring forth fruit in patience.

blocks.title: Parable of the Wheat and the Tares

texts.NIV84: Jesus told them another parable: "The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.
texts.KJV: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:
texts.NASB95: Jesus presented another parable to them, saying, “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a man who sowed good seed in his field.
texts.NKJV: Another parable He put forth to them, saying: “The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field;
texts.DRB: Another parable he proposed to them, saying: The kingdom of heaven is likened to a man that sowed good seeds in his field.

texts.NIV84: But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and went away.
texts.KJV: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.
texts.NASB95: But while his men were sleeping, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went away.
texts.NKJV: but while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat and went his way.
texts.DRB: But while men were asleep, his enemy came and oversowed cockle among the wheat and went his way.

texts.NIV84: When the wheat sprouted and formed heads, then the weeds also appeared.
texts.KJV: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
texts.NASB95: But when the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the tares became evident also.
texts.NKJV: But when the grain had sprouted and produced a crop, then the tares also appeared.
texts.DRB: And when the blade was sprung up, and had brought forth fruit, then appeared also the cockle.

texts.NIV84: "The owner's servants came to him and said, 'Sir, didn't you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?'
texts.KJV: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
texts.NASB95: The slaves of the landowner came and said to him, ‘Sir, did you not sow good seed in your field? How then does it have tares?’
texts.NKJV: So the servants of the owner came and said to him, ‘Sir, did you not sow good seed in your field? How then does it have tares?’
texts.DRB: And the servants of the goodman of the house coming said to him: Sir, didst thou not sow good seed in thy field? whence then hath it cockle?

texts.NIV84: "'An enemy did this,' he replied. "The servants asked him, 'Do you want us to go and pull them up?'
texts.KJV: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
texts.NASB95: And he said to them, ‘An enemy has done this!’ The slaves said to him, ‘Do you want us, then, to go and gather them up?’
texts.NKJV: He said to them, ‘An enemy has done this.’ The servants said to him, ‘Do you want us then to go and gather them up?’
texts.DRB: And he said to them: An enemy hath done this. And the servants said to him: Wilt thou that we go and gather it up?

texts.NIV84: "'No,' he answered, 'because while you are pulling the weeds, you may root up the wheat with them.
texts.KJV: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.
texts.NASB95: But he said, ‘No; for while you are gathering up the tares, you may uproot the wheat with them.
texts.NKJV: But he said, ‘No, lest while you gather up the tares you also uproot the wheat with them.
texts.DRB: And he said: No, lest perhaps gathering up the cockle, you root up the wheat also together with it.

texts.NIV84: Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat and bring it into my barn.'"
texts.KJV: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.
texts.NASB95: Allow both to grow together until the harvest; and in the time of the harvest I will say to the reapers, “First gather up the tares and bind them in bundles to burn them up; but gather the wheat into my barn.”’”
texts.NKJV: Let both grow together until the harvest, and at the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, “First gather together the tares and bind them in bundles to burn them, but gather the wheat into my barn.” ’ ”
texts.DRB: Suffer both to grow until the harvest, and in the time of the harvest I will say to the reapers: Gather up first the cockle, and bind it into bundles to burn, but the wheat gather ye into my barn.

texts.NIV84: Then he left the crowd and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, "Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field."
texts.KJV: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.
texts.NASB95: Then He left the crowds and went into the house. And His disciples came to Him and said, “Explain to us the parable of the tares of the field.”
texts.NKJV: Then Jesus sent the multitude away and went into the house. And His disciples came to Him, saying, “Explain to us the parable of the tares of the field.”
texts.DRB: Then having sent away the multitudes, he came into the house, and his disciples came to him, saying: Expound to us the parable of the cockle of the field.

texts.NIV84: He answered, "The one who sowed the good seed is the Son of Man.
texts.KJV: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
texts.NASB95: And He said, “The one who sows the good seed is the Son of Man,
texts.NKJV: He answered and said to them: “He who sows the good seed is the Son of Man.
texts.DRB: Who made answer and said to them: He that soweth the good seed, is the Son of man.

texts.NIV84: The field is the world, and the good seed stands for the sons of the kingdom. The weeds are the sons of the evil one,
texts.KJV: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;
texts.NASB95: and the field is the world; and as for the good seed, these are the sons of the kingdom; and the tares are the sons of the evil one;
texts.NKJV: The field is the world, the good seeds are the sons of the kingdom, but the tares are the sons of the wicked one.
texts.DRB: And the field, is the world. And the good seed are the children of the kingdom. And the cockle, are the children of the wicked one.

texts.NIV84: and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.
texts.KJV: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
texts.NASB95: and the enemy who sowed them is the devil, and the harvest is the end of the age; and the reapers are angels.
texts.NKJV: The enemy who sowed them is the devil, the harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are the angels.
texts.DRB: And the enemy that sowed them, is the devil. But the harvest is the end of the world. And the reapers are the angels.

texts.NIV84: "As the weeds are pulled up and burned in the fire, so it will be at the end of the age.
texts.KJV: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.
texts.NASB95: So just as the tares are gathered up and burned with fire, so shall it be at the end of the age.
texts.NKJV: Therefore as the tares are gathered and burned in the fire, so it will be at the end of this age.
texts.DRB: Even as cockle therefore is gathered up, and burnt with fire: so shall it be at the end of the world.

texts.NIV84: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will weed out of his kingdom everything that causes sin and all who do evil.
texts.KJV: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;
texts.NASB95: The Son of Man will send forth His angels, and they will gather out of His kingdom all stumbling blocks, and those who commit lawlessness,
texts.NKJV: The Son of Man will send out His angels, and they will gather out of His kingdom all things that offend, and those who practice lawlessness,
texts.DRB: The Son of man shall send his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all scandals, and them that work iniquity.

texts.NIV84: They will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.KJV: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
texts.NASB95: and will throw them into the furnace of fire; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
texts.NKJV: and will cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
texts.DRB: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

texts.NIV84: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears, let him hear.
texts.KJV: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
texts.NASB95: Then the righteous will shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears, let him hear.
texts.NKJV: Then the righteous will shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears to hear, let him hear!
texts.DRB: Then shall the just shine as the sun, in the kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

texts.NIV84: <span style='color:#952004; font-weight:600'>"Why do you call me, 'Lord, Lord,' and do not do what I say?<span>
texts.KJV: And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?
texts.NASB95: “Why do you call Me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and do not do what I say?
texts.NKJV: “But why do you call Me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and not do the things which I say?
texts.DRB: And why call you me, Lord, Lord; and do not the things which I say?

texts.NIV84: I will show you what he is like who comes to me and hears my words and puts them into practice.
texts.KJV: Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
texts.NASB95: Everyone who comes to Me and hears My words and acts on them, I will show you whom he is like:
texts.NKJV: Whoever comes to Me, and hears My sayings and does them, I will show you whom he is like:
texts.DRB: Every one that cometh to me, and heareth my words, and doth them, I will shew you to whom he is like.

texts.NIV84: He is like a man building a house, who dug down deep and laid the foundation on rock. When a flood came, the torrent struck that house but could not shake it, because it was well built.
texts.KJV: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.
texts.NASB95: he is like a man building a house, who dug deep and laid a foundation on the rock; and when a flood occurred, the torrent burst against that house and could not shake it, because it had been well built.
texts.NKJV: He is like a man building a house, who dug deep and laid the foundation on the rock. And when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently against that house, and could not shake it, for it was founded on the rock.
texts.DRB: He is like to a man building a house, who digged deep, and laid the foundation upon a rock. And when a flood came, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and it could not shake it; for it was founded on a rock.

texts.NIV84: But the one who hears my words and does not put them into practice is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. The moment the torrent struck that house, it collapsed and its destruction was complete."
texts.KJV: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
texts.NASB95: But the one who has heard and has not acted accordingly, is like a man who built a house on the ground without any foundation; and the torrent burst against it and immediately it collapsed, and the ruin of that house was great.”
texts.NKJV: But he who heard and did nothing is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the stream beat vehemently; and immediately it fell. And the ruin of that house was great.”
texts.DRB: But he that heareth, and doth not, is like to a man building his house upon the earth without a foundation: against which the stream beat vehemently, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.

texts.NIV84: "Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
texts.KJV: Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock:
texts.NASB95: “Therefore everyone who hears these words of Mine and acts on them, may be compared to a wise man who built his house on the rock.
texts.NKJV: “Therefore whoever hears these sayings of Mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house on the rock:
texts.DRB: Every one therefore that heareth these my words, and doth them, shall be likened to a wise man that built his house upon a rock,

texts.NIV84: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock.
texts.KJV: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.
texts.NASB95: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and slammed against that house; and yet it did not fall, for it had been founded on the rock.
texts.NKJV: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it did not fall, for it was founded on the rock.
texts.DRB: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and they beat upon that house, and it fell not, for it was founded on a rock.

texts.NIV84: But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
texts.KJV: And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:
texts.NASB95: Everyone who hears these words of Mine and does not act on them, will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
texts.NKJV: “But everyone who hears these sayings of Mine, and does not do them, will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand:
texts.DRB: And every one that heareth these my words, and doth them not, shall be like a foolish man that built his house upon the sand,

texts.NIV84: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell with a great crash."
texts.KJV: And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.
texts.NASB95: The rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and slammed against that house; and it fell—and great was its fall.”
texts.NKJV: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it fell. And great was its fall.”
texts.DRB: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and they beat upon that house, and it fell, and great was the fall thereof.

texts.NIV84: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching,
texts.KJV: And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended these sayings, the people were astonished at his doctrine:
texts.NASB95: When Jesus had finished these words, the crowds were amazed at His teaching;
texts.NKJV: And so it was, when Jesus had ended these sayings, that the people were astonished at His teaching,
texts.DRB: And it came to pass when Jesus had fully ended these words, the people were in admiration at his doctrine.

texts.NIV84: because he taught as one who had authority, and not as their teachers of the law.
texts.KJV: For he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
texts.NASB95: for He was teaching them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
texts.NKJV: for He taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
texts.DRB: For he was teaching them as one having power, and not as the scribes and Pharisees.

texts.NIV84: He said to them, "Do you bring in a lamp to put it under a bowl or a bed? Instead, don't you put it on its stand?
texts.KJV: And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
texts.NASB95: And He was saying to them, “A lamp is not brought to be put under a basket, is it, or under a bed? Is it not brought to be put on the lampstand?
texts.NKJV: Also He said to them, “Is a lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Is it not to be set on a lampstand?
texts.DRB: And he said to them: Doth a candle come in to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?

texts.NIV84: For whatever is hidden is meant to be disclosed, and whatever is concealed is meant to be brought out into the open.
texts.KJV: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
