texts.NASB95: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, then My servants would be fighting so that I would not be handed over to the Jews; but as it is, My kingdom is not of this realm.”
texts.NKJV: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, My servants would fight, so that I should not be delivered to the Jews; but now My kingdom is not from here.”
texts.DRB: Jesus answered: My kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would certainly strive that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now my kingdom is not from hence.

texts.NIV84: "You are a king, then!" said Pilate. Jesus answered, "You are right in saying I am a king. In fact, for this reason I was born, and for this I came into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone on the side of truth listens to me."
texts.KJV: Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.
texts.NASB95: Therefore Pilate said to Him, “So You are a king?” Jesus answered, “You say correctly that I am a king. For this I have been born, and for this I have come into the world, to testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears My voice.”
texts.NKJV: Pilate therefore said to Him, “Are You a king then?” Jesus answered, “You say rightly that I am a king. For this cause I was born, and for this cause I have come into the world, that I should bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears My voice.”
texts.DRB: Pilate therefore said to him: Art thou a king then? Jesus answered: Thou sayest that I am a king. For this was I born, and for this came I into the world; that I should give testimony to the truth. Every one that is of the truth, heareth my voice.

texts.NIV84: "What is truth?" Pilate asked. With this he went out again to the Jews and said, "I find no basis for a charge against him.
texts.KJV: Pilate saith unto him, What is truth? And when he had said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them, I find in him no fault at all.
texts.NASB95:
Pilate said to Him, “What is truth?”
And when he had said this, he went out again to the Jews and said to them, “I find no guilt in Him.
texts.NKJV: Pilate said to Him, “What is truth?” And when he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no fault in Him at all.
texts.DRB: Pilate saith to him: What is truth? And when he said this, he went out again to the Jews, and saith to them: I find no cause in him.

texts.NIV84: Whether you turn to the right or to the left, your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, "This is the way; walk in it."
texts.KJV: And thine ears shall hear a word behind thee, saying, This is the way, walk ye in it, when ye turn to the right hand, and when ye turn to the left.
texts.NASB95: Your ears will hear a word behind you, “This is the way, walk in it,” whenever you turn to the right or to the left.
texts.NKJV:
Your ears shall hear a word behind you, saying,
“This is the way, walk in it,”
Whenever you turn to the right hand
Or whenever you turn to the left.
texts.DRB: And thy ears shall hear the word of one admonishing thee behind thy back: This is the way, walk ye in it: and go not aside neither to the right hand, nor to the left.

blocks.title: Listen to the Fear of the Lord

texts.NIV84: Come, my children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
texts.KJV: Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
texts.NASB95:
Come, you children, listen to me;
I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
texts.NKJV:
Come, you children, listen to me;
I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
texts.DRB: Come, children, hearken to me: I will teach you the fear of the Lord.

texts.NIV84: Whoever of you loves life and desires to see many good days,
texts.KJV: What man is he that desireth life, and loveth many days, that he may see good?
texts.NASB95:
Who is the man who desires life
And loves length of days that he may see good?
texts.NKJV:
Who is the man who desires life,
And loves many days, that he may see good?
texts.DRB: Who is the man that desireth life: who loveth to see good days?

texts.NIV84: keep your tongue from evil and your lips from speaking lies.
texts.KJV: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.
texts.NASB95:
Keep your tongue from evil
And your lips from speaking deceit.
texts.NKJV:
Keep your tongue from evil,
And your lips from speaking deceit.
texts.DRB: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.

texts.NIV84: Turn from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
texts.KJV: Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
texts.NASB95:
Depart from evil and do good;
Seek peace and pursue it.
texts.NKJV:
Depart from evil and do good;
Seek peace and pursue it.
texts.DRB: Turn away from evil and do good: seek after peace and pursue it.

texts.NIV84: Hear this, all you peoples; listen, all who live in this world,
texts.KJV: Hear this, all ye people; give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world:
texts.NASB95:
Hear this, all peoples;
Give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
texts.NKJV:
Hear this, all peoples;
Give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
texts.DRB: Unto the end, a psalm for the sons of Core. Hear these things, all ye nations: give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world.

texts.NIV84: both low and high, rich and poor alike:
texts.KJV: Both low and high, rich and poor, together.
texts.NASB95:
Both low and high,
Rich and poor together.
texts.NKJV:
Both low and high,
Rich and poor together.
texts.DRB: All you that are earthborn, and you sons of men: both rich and poor together.

texts.NIV84: My mouth will speak words of wisdom; the utterance from my heart will give understanding.
texts.KJV: My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
texts.NASB95:
My mouth will speak wisdom,
And the meditation of my heart will be understanding.
texts.NKJV:
My mouth shall speak wisdom,
And the meditation of my heart shall give understanding.
texts.DRB: My mouth shall speak wisdom: and the meditation of my heart understanding.

texts.NIV84: I will turn my ear to a proverb; with the harp I will expound my riddle:
texts.KJV: I will incline mine ear to a parable: I will open my dark saying upon the harp.
texts.NASB95:
I will incline my ear to a proverb;
I will express my riddle on the harp.
texts.NKJV:
I will incline my ear to a proverb;
I will disclose my dark saying on the harp.
texts.DRB: I will incline my ear to a parable; I will open my proposition on the psaltery.

texts.NIV84: Why should I fear when evil days come, when wicked deceivers surround me-
texts.KJV: Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, when the iniquity of my heels shall compass me about?
texts.NASB95:
Why should I fear in days of adversity,
When the iniquity of my foes surrounds me,
texts.NKJV:
Why should I fear in the days of evil,
When the iniquity at my heels surrounds me?
texts.DRB: Why shall I fear in the evil day? the iniquity of my heel shall encompass me.

texts.NIV84: those who trust in their wealth and boast of their great riches?
texts.KJV: They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;
texts.NASB95:
Even those who trust in their wealth
And boast in the abundance of their riches?
texts.NKJV:
Those who trust in their wealth
And boast in the multitude of their riches,
texts.DRB: They that trust in their own strength, and glory in the multitude of their riches,

texts.NIV84: No man can redeem the life of another or give to God a ransom for him-
texts.KJV: None of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him:
texts.NASB95:
No man can by any means redeem his brother
Or give to God a ransom for him—
texts.NKJV:
None of them can by any means redeem his brother,
Nor give to God a ransom for him—
texts.DRB: No brother can redeem, nor shall man redeem: he shall not give to God his ransom,

texts.NIV84: the ransom for a life is costly, no payment is ever enough-
texts.KJV: (For the redemption of their soul is precious, and it ceaseth for ever:)
texts.NASB95:
For the redemption of his soul is costly,
And he should cease trying forever—
texts.NKJV:
For the redemption of their souls is costly,
And it shall cease forever—
texts.DRB: Nor the price of the redemption of his soul: and shall labour for ever,

texts.NIV84: that he should live on forever and not see decay.
texts.KJV: That he should still live for ever, and not see corruption.
texts.NASB95:
That he should live on eternally,
That he should not undergo decay.
texts.NKJV:
That he should continue to live eternally,
And not see the Pit.
texts.DRB: and shall still live unto the end.

texts.NIV84: For all can see that wise men die; the foolish and the senseless alike perish and leave their wealth to others.
texts.KJV: For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.
texts.DRB: He shall not see destruction, when he shall see the wise dying: the senseless and the fool shall perish together: And they shall leave their riches to strangers:

texts.NIV84: Their tombs will remain their houses forever, their dwellings for endless generations, though they had named lands after themselves.
texts.KJV: Their inward thought is, that their houses shall continue for ever, and their dwelling places to all generations; they call their lands after their own names.
texts.DRB: and their sepulchres shall be their houses for ever. Their dwelling places to all generations: they have called their lands by their names.

texts.NIV84: I will listen to what God the LORD will say; he promises peace to his people, his saints- but let them not return to folly.
texts.KJV: I will hear what God the Lord will speak: for he will speak peace unto his people, and to his saints: but let them not turn again to folly.
texts.NKJV:
I will hear what God the Lord will speak,
For He will speak peace
To His people and to His saints;
But let them not turn back to folly.
texts.DRB: I will hear what the Lord God will speak in me: for he will speak peace unto his people: And unto his saints: and unto them that are converted to the heart.

texts.NIV84: Surely his salvation is near those who fear him, that his glory may dwell in our land.
texts.KJV: Surely his salvation is nigh them that fear him; that glory may dwell in our land.
texts.NASB95:
Surely His salvation is near to those who fear Him,
That glory may dwell in our land.
texts.NKJV:
Surely His salvation is near to those who fear Him,
That glory may dwell in our land.
texts.DRB: Surely his salvation is near to them that fear him: that glory may dwell in our land.

texts.NIV84: Guard your steps when you go to the house of God. Go near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools, who do not know that they do wrong.
texts.KJV: Keep thy foot when thou goest to the house of God, and be more ready to hear, than to give the sacrifice of fools: for they consider not that they do evil.
texts.NASB95: Guard your steps as you go to the house of God and draw near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools; for they do not know they are doing evil.
texts.NKJV: Walk prudently when you go to the house of God; and draw near to hear rather than to give the sacrifice of fools, for they do not know that they do evil.
texts.DRB: Keep thy foot, when thou goest into the house of God, and draw nigh to hear. For much better is obedience, than the victims of fools, who know not what evil they do.

texts.NIV84: Do not be quick with your mouth, do not be hasty in your heart to utter anything before God. God is in heaven and you are on earth, so let your words be few.
texts.KJV: Be not rash with thy mouth, and let not thine heart be hasty to utter any thing before God: for God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.
texts.NASB95: Do not be hasty in word or impulsive in thought to bring up a matter in the presence of God. For God is in heaven and you are on the earth; therefore let your words be few.
texts.NKJV:
Do not be rash with your mouth,
And let not your heart utter anything hastily before God.
For God is in heaven, and you on earth;
Therefore let your words be few.
texts.DRB: Speak not any thing rashly, and let not thy heart be hasty to utter a word before God. For God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.

texts.NIV84: As a dream comes when there are many cares, so the speech of a fool when there are many words.
texts.KJV: For a dream cometh through the multitude of business; and a fool's voice is known by multitude of words.
texts.NASB95: For the dream comes through much effort and the voice of a fool through many words.
texts.NKJV:
For a dream comes through much activity,
And a fool’s voice is known by his many words.
texts.DRB: Dreams follow many cares: and in many words shall be found folly.

texts.NIV84: When you make a vow to God, do not delay in fulfilling it. He has no pleasure in fools; fulfill your vow.
texts.KJV: When thou vowest a vow unto God, defer not to pay it; for he hath no pleasure in fools: pay that which thou hast vowed.
texts.NASB95: When you make a vow to God, do not be late in paying it; for He takes no delight in fools. Pay what you vow!
texts.DRB: If thou hast vowed any thing to God, defer not to pay it: for an unfaithful and foolish promise displeaseth him: but whatsoever thou hast vowed, pay it.

texts.NIV84: It is better not to vow than to make a vow and not fulfill it.
texts.KJV: Better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than that thou shouldest vow and not pay.
texts.NASB95: It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
texts.NKJV: Better not to vow than to vow and not pay.
texts.DRB: And it is much better not to vow, than after a vow not to perform the things promised.

texts.NIV84: Do not let your mouth lead you into sin. And do not protest to the temple messenger, "My vow was a mistake." Why should God be angry at what you say and destroy the work of your hands?
texts.KJV: Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel, that it was an error: wherefore should God be angry at thy voice, and destroy the work of thine hands?
texts.NASB95: Do not let your speech cause you to sin and do not say in the presence of the messenger of God that it was a mistake. Why should God be angry on account of your voice and destroy the work of your hands?
texts.NKJV: Do not let your mouth cause your flesh to sin, nor say before the messenger of God that it was an error. Why should God be angry at your excuse and destroy the work of your hands?
texts.DRB: Give not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin: and say not before the angel: There is no providence: lest God be angry at thy words, and destroy all the works of thy hands.

texts.NIV84: Much dreaming and many words are meaningless. Therefore stand in awe of God.
texts.KJV: For in the multitude of dreams and many words there are also divers vanities: but fear thou God.
texts.NASB95: For in many dreams and in many words there is emptiness. Rather, fear God.
texts.NKJV: For in the multitude of dreams and many words there is also vanity. But fear God.
texts.DRB: Where there are many dreams, there are many vanities, and words without number: but do thou fear God.

texts.NIV84: My son, if you accept my words and store up my commands within you,
texts.KJV: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;
texts.NASB95:
My son, if you will receive my words
And treasure my commandments within you,
texts.NKJV:
My son, if you receive my words,
And treasure my commands within you,
texts.DRB: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and wilt hide my commandments with thee,

texts.NIV84: turning your ear to wisdom and applying your heart to understanding,
texts.KJV: So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
texts.NASB95:
Make your ear attentive to wisdom,
Incline your heart to understanding;
texts.NKJV:
So that you incline your ear to wisdom,
And apply your heart to understanding;
texts.DRB: That thy ear may hearken to wisdom: Incline thy heart to know prudence:

texts.NIV84: and if you call out for insight and cry aloud for understanding,
texts.KJV: Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, and liftest up thy voice for understanding;
texts.NASB95:
For if you cry for discernment,
Lift your voice for understanding;
texts.NKJV:
Yes, if you cry out for discernment,
And lift up your voice for understanding,
texts.DRB: For if thou shalt call for wisdom, and incline thy heart to prudence:

texts.NIV84: and if you look for it as for silver and search for it as for hidden treasure,
texts.KJV: If thou seekest her as silver, and searchest for her as for hid treasures;
texts.NASB95:
If you seek her as silver
And search for her as for hidden treasures;
texts.NKJV:
If you seek her as silver,
And search for her as for hidden treasures;
texts.DRB: If thou shalt seek her as money, and shalt dig for her as for a treasure:

texts.NIV84: then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.
texts.KJV: Then shalt thou understand the fear of the Lord, and find the knowledge of God.
texts.NASB95:
Then you will discern the fear of the Lord
And discover the knowledge of God.
texts.NKJV:
Then you will understand the fear of the Lord,
And find the knowledge of God.
texts.DRB: Then shalt thou understand the fear of the Lord, and shalt find the knowledge of God.

texts.NIV84: For the LORD gives wisdom, and from his mouth come knowledge and understanding.
texts.KJV: For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
texts.NASB95:
For the Lord gives wisdom;
From His mouth come knowledge and understanding.
texts.NKJV:
For the Lord gives wisdom;
From His mouth come knowledge and understanding;
texts.DRB: Because the Lord giveth wisdom: and out of his mouth cometh prudence and knowledge.

texts.NIV84: He holds victory in store for the upright, he is a shield to those whose walk is blameless,
texts.KJV: He layeth up sound wisdom for the righteous: he is a buckler to them that walk uprightly.
texts.NASB95:
He stores up sound wisdom for the upright;
He is a shield to those who walk in integrity,
texts.NKJV:
He stores up sound wisdom for the upright;
He is a shield to those who walk uprightly;
texts.DRB: He will keep the salvation of the righteous, and protect them that walk in simplicity.

texts.NIV84: for he guards the course of the just and protects the way of his faithful ones.
texts.KJV: He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.
texts.NASB95:
Guarding the paths of justice,
And He preserves the way of His godly ones.
texts.NKJV:
He guards the paths of justice,
And preserves the way of His saints.
texts.DRB: Keeping the paths of justice, and guarding the ways of saints.

blocks.title: Incline Your Ear to My Sayings

texts.NIV84: My son, pay attention to what I say; listen closely to my words.
texts.KJV: My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.
texts.NASB95:
My son, give attention to my words;
Incline your ear to my sayings.
texts.NKJV:
My son, give attention to my words;
Incline your ear to my sayings.
texts.DRB: My son, hearken to my words, and incline thy ear to my sayings.

texts.NIV84: Do not let them out of your sight, keep them within your heart;
texts.KJV: Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
texts.NASB95:
Do not let them depart from your sight;
Keep them in the midst of your heart.
texts.NKJV:
Do not let them depart from your eyes;
Keep them in the midst of your heart;
texts.DRB: Let them not depart from thy eyes, keep them in the midst of thy heart:

texts.NIV84: for they are life to those who find them and health to a man's whole body.
texts.KJV: For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.
texts.NASB95:
For they are life to those who find them
And health to all their body.
texts.NKJV:
For they are life to those who find them,
And health to all their flesh.
texts.DRB: For they are life to those that find them, and health to all flesh.

texts.NIV84: Above all else, guard your heart, for it is the wellspring of life.
texts.KJV: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
texts.NASB95:
Watch over your heart with all diligence,
For from it flow the springs of life.
texts.NKJV:
Keep your heart with all diligence,
For out of it spring the issues of life.
texts.DRB: With all watchfulness keep thy heart, because life issueth out from it.

texts.NIV84: Put away perversity from your mouth; keep corrupt talk far from your lips.
texts.KJV: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee.
texts.NASB95:
Put away from you a deceitful mouth
And put devious speech far from you.
texts.NKJV:
Put away from you a deceitful mouth,
And put perverse lips far from you.
texts.DRB: Remove from thee a froward mouth, and let detracting lips be far from thee.

texts.NIV84: Let your eyes look straight ahead, fix your gaze directly before you.
texts.KJV: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
texts.NASB95:
Let your eyes look directly ahead
And let your gaze be fixed straight in front of you.
texts.NKJV:
Let your eyes look straight ahead,
And your eyelids look right before you.
texts.DRB: Let thy eyes look straight on, and let thy eyelids go before thy steps.

texts.NIV84: Make level paths for your feet and take only ways that are firm.
texts.KJV: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
texts.NASB95:
Watch the path of your feet
And all your ways will be established.
texts.NKJV:
Ponder the path of your feet,
And let all your ways be established.
texts.DRB: Make straight the path for thy feet, and all thy ways shall be established.

texts.NIV84: Do not swerve to the right or the left; keep your foot from evil.
texts.KJV: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil.
texts.NASB95:
Do not turn to the right nor to the left;
Turn your foot from evil.
texts.NKJV:
Do not turn to the right or the left;
Remove your foot from evil.
texts.DRB: Decline not to the right hand, nor to the left: turn away thy foot from evil. For the Lord knoweth the ways that are on the right hand: but those are perverse which are on the left hand. But he will make thy courses straight, he will bring forward thy ways in peace.

texts.NIV84: Listen, for I have worthy things to say; I open my lips to speak what is right.
texts.KJV: Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.
texts.NASB95:
“Listen, for I will speak noble things;
And the opening of my lips will reveal right things.
texts.NKJV:
Listen, for I will speak of excellent things,
And from the opening of my lips will come right things;
texts.DRB: Hear, for I will speak of great things: and my lips shall be opened to preach right things.

texts.NIV84: My mouth speaks what is true, for my lips detest wickedness.
texts.KJV: For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
texts.NASB95:
“For my mouth will utter truth;
And wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
texts.NKJV:
For my mouth will speak truth;
Wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
texts.DRB: My mouth shall meditate truth, and my lips shall hate wickedness.

texts.NIV84: All the words of my mouth are just; none of them is crooked or perverse.
texts.KJV: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing froward or perverse in them.
texts.NASB95:
“All the utterances of my mouth are in righteousness;
There is nothing crooked or perverted in them.
texts.NKJV:
All the words of my mouth are with righteousness;
Nothing crooked or perverse is in them.
texts.DRB: All my words are just, there is nothing wicked nor perverse in them.

texts.NIV84: To the discerning all of them are right; they are faultless to those who have knowledge.
texts.KJV: They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.
texts.NASB95:
“They are all straightforward to him who understands,
And right to those who find knowledge.
texts.NKJV:
They are all plain to him who understands,
And right to those who find knowledge.
texts.DRB: They are right to them that understand, and just to them that find knowledge.

texts.NIV84: Choose my instruction instead of silver, knowledge rather than choice gold,
texts.KJV: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.
texts.NASB95:
“Take my instruction and not silver,
And knowledge rather than choicest gold.
texts.NKJV:
Receive my instruction, and not silver,
And knowledge rather than choice gold;
texts.DRB: Receive my instruction, and not money: choose knowledge rather than gold.

texts.NIV84: for wisdom is more precious than rubies, and nothing you desire can compare with her.
texts.KJV: For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.
texts.NASB95:
“For wisdom is better than jewels;
And all desirable things cannot compare with her.
texts.NKJV:
For wisdom is better than rubies,
And all the things one may desire cannot be compared with her.
texts.DRB: 11For wisdom is better than all the most precious things: and whatsoever may be desired cannot be compared to

texts.NIV84: "Now then, my sons, listen to me; blessed are those who keep my ways.
texts.KJV: Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways.
texts.NASB95:
“Now therefore, O sons, listen to me,
For blessed are they who keep my ways.
texts.NKJV:
“Now therefore, listen to me, my children,
For blessed are those who keep my ways.
texts.DRB: Now therefore, ye children, hear me: Blessed are they that keep my ways.

texts.NIV84: Listen to my instruction and be wise; do not ignore it.
texts.KJV: Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.
texts.NASB95:
“Heed instruction and be wise,
And do not neglect it.
texts.NKJV:
Hear instruction and be wise,
And do not disdain it.
texts.DRB: Hear instruction and be wise, and refuse it not.

texts.NIV84: Blessed is the man who listens to me, watching daily at my doors, waiting at my doorway.
texts.KJV: Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.
texts.NASB95:
“Blessed is the man who listens to me,
Watching daily at my gates,
Waiting at my doorposts.
texts.NKJV:
Blessed is the man who listens to me,
Watching daily at my gates,
Waiting at the posts of my doors.
texts.DRB: Blessed is the man that heareth me, and that watcheth daily at my gates, and waiteth at the posts of my doors.

texts.NIV84: For whoever finds me finds life and receives favor from the LORD.
texts.KJV: For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the Lord.
texts.NASB95:
“For he who finds me finds life
And obtains favor from the Lord.
texts.NKJV:
For whoever finds me finds life,
And obtains favor from the Lord;
texts.DRB: He that shall find me, shall find life, and shall have salvation from the Lord:

texts.NIV84: But whoever fails to find me harms himself; all who hate me love death."
texts.KJV: But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: all they that hate me love death.
texts.NASB95:
“But he who sins against me injures himself;
All those who hate me love death.”
texts.NKJV:
But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul;
All those who hate me love death.”
texts.DRB: But he that shall sin against me, shall hurt his own soul. All that hate me love death.

texts.NIV84: He who answers before listening- that is his folly and his shame.
texts.KJV: He that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly and shame unto him.
texts.NASB95:
He who gives an answer before he hears,
It is folly and shame to him.
texts.NKJV:
He who answers a matter before he hears it,
It is folly and shame to him.
texts.DRB: He that answereth before he heareth sheweth himself to be a fool, and worthy of confusion.

texts.NIV84: Listen to advice and accept instruction, and in the end you will be wise.
texts.KJV: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
texts.NASB95:
Listen to counsel and accept discipline,
That you may be wise the rest of your days.
texts.NKJV:
Listen to counsel and receive instruction,
That you may be wise in your latter days.
texts.DRB: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayst be wise in thy latter end.

blocks.title: Incline Your Ear and Hear the Words

texts.NIV84: Pay attention and listen to the sayings of the wise; apply your heart to what I teach,
texts.KJV: Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.
texts.NASB95:
Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise,
And apply your mind to my knowledge;
texts.NKJV:
Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise,
And apply your heart to my knowledge;
texts.DRB: Incline thy ear, and hear the words of the wise: and apply thy heart to my doctrine :

texts.NIV84: for it is pleasing when you keep them in your heart and have all of them ready on your lips.
texts.KJV: For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.
texts.NASB95:
For it will be pleasant if you keep them within you,
That they may be ready on your lips.
texts.NKJV:
For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you;
Let them all be fixed upon your lips,
texts.DRB: Which shall be beautiful for thee, if thou keep it in thy bowels, and it shall flow in thy lips:

texts.NIV84: So that your trust may be in the LORD, I teach you today, even you.
texts.KJV: That thy trust may be in the Lord, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.
texts.NASB95:
So that your trust may be in the Lord,
I have taught you today, even you.
texts.NKJV:
So that your trust may be in the Lord;
I have instructed you today, even you.
texts.DRB: That thy trust may be in the Lord, wherefore I have also shewn it to thee this day.

texts.NIV84: Have I not written thirty sayings for you, sayings of counsel and knowledge,
texts.KJV: Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,
texts.NASB95:
Have I not written to you excellent things
Of counsels and knowledge,
texts.NKJV:
Have I not written to you excellent things
Of counsels and knowledge,
texts.DRB: Behold I have described it to thee three manner of ways, in thoughts and knowledge :

texts.NIV84: teaching you true and reliable words, so that you can give sound answers to him who sent you?
texts.KJV: That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?
texts.NASB95:
To make you know the certainty of the words of truth
That you may correctly answer him who sent you?
texts.NKJV:
That I may make you know the certainty of the words of truth,
That you may answer words of truth
To those who send to you?
texts.DRB: That I might shew thee the certainty, and the words of truth, to answer out of these to them that sent thee.

blocks.title: A Wise Reprover to an Obedient Ear

texts.NIV84: Like an earring of gold or an ornament of fine gold is a wise man's rebuke to a listening ear.
texts.KJV: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover upon an obedient ear.
texts.NASB95:
Like an earring of gold and an ornament of fine gold
Is a wise reprover to a listening ear.
texts.NKJV:
Like an earring of gold and an ornament of fine gold
Is a wise rebuker to an obedient ear.
texts.DRB: As an earring of gold and a bright pearl, so is he that reproveth the wise, and the obedient ear.
